Name: USkeletalMesh
Description:
SkeletalMesh is geometry bound to a hierarchical skeleton of bones which can be animated for the purpose of deforming the mesh.
  Skeletal Meshes are built up of two parts; a set of polygons composed to make up the surface of the mesh, and a hierarchical skeleton which can be used to animate the polygons.
  The 3D models, rigging, and animations are created in an external modeling and animation application (3DSMax, Maya, Softimage, etc).

Variables.Name: Skeleton
Variables.Description: Skeleton of this skeletal mesh

Variables.Name: bForceAllowCPUAccess
Variables.Description: Ignore "FreeSkeletalMeshBuffers" console value.

Variables.Name: ImportedBounds
Variables.Description: Original imported mesh bounds

Variables.Name: ExtendedBounds
Variables.Description: Bounds extended by user values below

Variables.Name: PositiveBoundsExtension
Variables.Description:
Bound extension values in addition to imported bound in the positive direction of XYZ, 
	 	positive value increases bound size and negative value decreases bound size. 
	 	The final bound would be from [Imported Bound - Negative Bound] to [Imported Bound + Positive Bound].

Variables.Name: NegativeBoundsExtension
Variables.Description:
Bound extension values in addition to imported bound in the negative direction of XYZ, 
	 	positive value increases bound size and negative value decreases bound size. 
	 	The final bound would be from [Imported Bound - Negative Bound] to [Imported Bound + Positive Bound].

Variables.Name: bIsStreamable
Variables.Description: Streamable flag, determine whether to split the lod serialization, WITH_STREAMING_SM_LOD

Variables.Name: Materials
Variables.Description: List of materials applied to this mesh.

Variables.Name: ReplaceMaterialInterface
Variables.Description: Replace for async compile pso.

Variables.Name: SkelMirrorTable
Variables.Description: List of bones that should be mirrored.

Variables.Name: CullingScreenSize
Variables.Description: Culling screen size

Variables.Name: LODInfo
Variables.Description: Struct containing information for each LOD level, such as materials to use, and when use the LOD.

Variables.Name: PerLODBiasTypeInfo
Variables.Description: When autonomous or simulated pawn needs special LOD bias

Variables.Name: bUseFullPrecisionUVs
Variables.Description: If true, use 32 bit UVs. If false, use 16 bit UVs to save memory

Variables.Name: bUsedWithDynamicInstancing
Variables.Description: Whether or not this mesh can be used with dynamic instancing.

Variables.Name: bHasBeenSimplified
Variables.Description: true if this mesh has ever been simplified with Simplygon.

Variables.Name: bHasVertexColors
Variables.Description: Whether or not the mesh has vertex colors

Variables.Name: bEnablePerPolyCollision
Variables.Description: Uses skinned data for collision data. Per poly collision cannot be used for simulation, in most cases you are better off using the physics asset

Variables.Name: bEnableSelfCollision
Variables.Description: Need self-collision in an aggregate. In most cases you don't need if the aggregate isn't containing the ragdoll. submit by elvisxu

Variables.Name: PhysicsAsset
Variables.Description:
Physics and collision information used for this USkeletalMesh, set up in Physics Asset Editor.
	 	This is used for per-bone hit detection, accurate bounding box calculation and ragdoll physics for example.

Variables.Name: ShadowPhysicsAsset
Variables.Description:
Physics asset whose shapes will be used for shadowing when components have bCastCharacterCapsuleDirectShadow or bCastCharacterCapsuleIndirectShadow enabled.
	  Only spheres and sphyl shapes in the physics asset can be supported.  The more shapes used, the higher the cost of the capsule shadows will be.

Variables.Name: NodeMappingData
Variables.Description: Mapping data that is saved

Variables.Name: LodModelsHasSkinweight
Variables.Description: use for FStaticLODModel Serialize SkinweightProfilesData

Variables.Name: ClothingAssets_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: Legacy clothing asset data, will be converted to new assets after loading

Variables.Name: PostProcessAnimBlueprint
Variables.Description:
Animation Blueprint class to run as a post process for this mesh.
	   This blueprint will be ran before physics, but after the main
	   anim instance for any skeletal mesh component using this mesh.

Variables.Name: MeshClothingAssets
Variables.Description:
Clothing assets imported to this mesh. May or may not be in use currently on the mesh.
	  Ordering not guaranteed, use the provided getters to access elements in this array
	  whenever possible

Variables.Name: AssetUserData
Variables.Description: Array of user data stored with the asset

Variables.Name: Sockets
Variables.Description:
Array of named socket locations, set up in editor and used as a shortcut instead of specifying 
	 	everything explicitly to AttachComponent in the SkeletalMeshComponent.

Variables.Name: SkinWeightProfiles
Variables.Description: Set of skin weight profiles associated with this mesh

Variables.Name: ScreenSizeCullingRoughDistance
Variables.Description: Rough Distance of Screen size Culling

Variables.Name: AssetImportData
Variables.Description: Importing data and options used for this mesh

Variables.Name: SourceFilePath_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: Path to the resource used to construct this skeletal mesh

Variables.Name: SourceFileTimestamp_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: DateTime-stamp of the file from the last import

Variables.Name: ThumbnailInfo
Variables.Description: Information for thumbnail rendering

Variables.Name: bHasCustomDefaultEditorCamera
Variables.Description: Should we use a custom camera transform when viewing this mesh in the tools

Variables.Name: DefaultEditorCameraLocation
Variables.Description: Default camera location

Variables.Name: DefaultEditorCameraRotation
Variables.Description: Default camera rotation

Variables.Name: DefaultEditorCameraLookAt
Variables.Description: Default camera look at

Variables.Name: DefaultEditorCameraOrthoZoom
Variables.Description: Default camera ortho zoom

Variables.Name: OptimizationSettings
Variables.Description: Optimization settings used to simplify LODs of this mesh.

Variables.Name: PreviewAttachedAssetContainer
Variables.Description: Attached assets component for this mesh

Variables.Name: SelectedEditorSection
Variables.Description: The section currently selected in the Editor. Used for highlighting

Variables.Name: SelectedEditorMaterial
Variables.Description: The Material currently selected. need to remember this index for reimporting cloth

Variables.Name: SelectedClothingSection
Variables.Description: The section currently selected for clothing. need to remember this index for reimporting cloth

Variables.Name: FloorOffset
Variables.Description: Height offset for the floor mesh in the editor

Variables.Name: RetargetBasePose
Variables.Description: This is buffer that saves pose that is used by retargeting

Functions.Name: GetBounds
Functions.Description: Get the extended bounds of this mesh (imported bounds plus bounds extension)

Functions.Name: GetImportedBounds
Functions.Description: Get the original imported bounds of the skel mesh

Functions.Name: FindSocket
Functions.Description:
Find a socket object in this SkeletalMesh by name. 
	 	Entering NAME_None will return NULL. If there are multiple sockets with the same name, will return the first one.

Functions.Name: FindSocketAndIndex
Functions.Description:
Find a socket object in this SkeletalMesh by name.
		Entering NAME_None will return NULL. If there are multiple sockets with the same name, will return the first one.
	   Also returns the index for the socket allowing for future fast access via GetSocketByIndex()

Functions.Name: NumSockets
Functions.Description: Returns the number of sockets available. Both on this mesh and it's skeleton.

Functions.Name: GetSocketByIndex
Functions.Description: Returns a socket by index. Max index is NumSockets(). The meshes sockets are accessed first, then the skeletons.

Functions.Name: IsSectionUsingCloth
Functions.Description:
Checks whether the provided section is using APEX cloth. if bCheckCorrespondingSections is true
	  disabled sections will defer to correspond sections to see if they use cloth (non-cloth sections
	  are disabled and another section added when cloth is enabled, using this flag allows for a check
	  on the original section to succeed)

Params.Name: bCheckCorrespondingSections
Params.Description: Whether to check corresponding sections for disabled sections
